channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Top 10 EPL’s most expensive signings since 2016
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Discover the top 10 EPL most expensive signings since 2016, featuring record-breaking transfers like Caicedo, Enzo, and Isak. See who made the list!
Read More: https://punchng.com/top-10-epls-most-expensive-signings-since-2016/

item.title: Full text of Trump’s commendation letter to Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Read the full text of Donald Trump’s letter commending President Bola Tinubu’s decisive leadership in tackling terrorism and insecurity in Nigeria.
Read More: https://punchng.com/full-text-of-trumps-commendation-letter-to-tinubu/

item.title: Cut smartphone taxes to bridge digital divide, GSMA urges African nations
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
GSMA urges African nations, including Nigeria, to cut smartphone taxes and reform regulations to bridge the digital divide and boost digital inclusion.
Read More: https://punchng.com/cut-smartphone-taxes-to-bridge-to-digital-divide-gsma-urges-african-nations/

item.title: CAF boss Motsepe rules out South Africa presidential bid
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
CAF President Patrice Motsepe dismisses speculation of a future South Africa presidential bid, stating his focus remains on African football.
Read More: https://punchng.com/caf-boss-motsepe-rules-out-south-africa-presidential-bid/

item.title: Police arrest two suspected car thieves in Plateau, recover vehicle
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Plateau State Police Command arrests two suspected car thieves and recovers a stolen Toyota Hilux, tracking the vehicle to Bauchi State.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-two-suspected-car-thieves-in-plateau-recover-vehicle/

item.title: Alaafin relaxes Oro movement restriction for female students sitting BECE

item.description:
The Alaafin of Oyo has modified the Oro Festival movement restriction, allowing female students to sit for the Basic Education Certificate Examination in O

item.title: Inter Miami sign Casemiro on free transfer
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Brazilian midfielder Casemiro signs with Inter Miami on a free transfer after leaving Manchester United, aiming for continued growth and success in MLS.
Read More: https://punchng.com/inter-miami-sign-casemiro-on-free-transfer/

item.title: Ronaldo misses out as FIFA unveils World Cup Best XI
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Spain leads with three players in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Best XI, as Ronaldo misses out. See the full team featuring Messi, Mbappé, and Haaland.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ronaldo-misses-out-as-fifa-unveils-world-cup-best-xi/

item.title: Spain’s goalkeeper sets World Cup record with seven clean sheets
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Spain’s Unai Simón made history at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, setting a new record with seven clean sheets. Discover his pivotal role in Spain’s triumph.
Read More: https://punchng.com/spains-goalkeeper-sets-world-cup-record-with-seven-clean-sheets/

item.title: Nigeria leads in online gambling regulation as illegal market hits $17.8bn
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The report, the first comprehensive assessment of online gambling across all 54 African countries, showed that Africa's online gambling Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR) reached $23 billion in 2025. item.title: FCMB moves to unlock capital for healthcare growth
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Speaking ahead of the summit, MD/CEO of First City Monument Bank, Yemisi Edun, said healthcare is critical to Nigeria's economic development and that sustainable healthcare delivery requires collaboration across the public and private sectors.

item.title: Ipswich Seal Deal For Morocco Defender Issa Diop
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Ipswich Town have completed the signing of Morocco international defender Issa Diop from Premier League rivals Fulham for an undisclosed fee, the club confirmed. The 29-year-old centre-back has committed to a long-term deal at Portman Road that runs until the summer of 2030, becoming the latest addi

item.title: Hitler’s Austrian Birthplace Transformed into Police Station
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Austria opened a new police station in Adolf Hitler’s former house on Wednesday, in the hope of deterring today’s ascendant far-right extremists from making the pilgrimage to the Nazi dictator’s birthplace. In a ceremony to mark the opening, Johannes Waidbacher, mayor of the small

item.title: Adamawa: Supporters protest against plans to replace ADC governorship candidate
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The protesters marched to the party’s state Secretariat, on Wednesday in Yola, carrying placards and demanding that the candidate remain the party’s authentic flagbearer. item.title: US Court Sets June 2027 Trial For Ousted Venezuelan President Maduro, Wife
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A United States court has scheduled the trial of Venezuela’s ousted president, Nicolas Maduro, and his wife, Cilia Flores, for June 1, 2027, following their arrest by US forces in January. Maduro, 63, and Flores have been held in a Brooklyn detention facility for more than seven months after pleadin

item.title: 7 Reasons Nigerians are selling Bitcoin on Apexpay
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The rate you see is the rate you get. Apexpay does not charge a hidden platform fee, so what the rate calculator shows you before the trade is what enters your account after it.

item.title: Foden Commits Future To Manchester City With New Deal Until 2030

item.description: Phil Foden has ended speculation over his future by signing a new four-year contract with Manchester City, keeping him at the Etihad Stadium until the summer of 2030. The England midfielder’s previous deal was due to expire next year, but the new agreement underlines City’s long-term faith in one of

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Southern Brave captain Sophie Molineux starred by taking three wickets to lead her side to a thrilling eight-run victory against Welsh Fire Women in The Hundred on Wednesday.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: July is one of the wettest months in Lagos. item.title: “Idol-free palaces are purer than mosques, churches” — Oluwo
item.dc:creator: The Sun

The flag-off ceremony was held in Wamakko town, the headquarters of Wamakko Local Government Area, on Wednesday.

item.title: Nigeria bonds: Top issuers paid up to 20% to raise debt in H1 2026

item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission has identified 908 suspected ghost workers across at least 50 federal ministries, departments and agencies, recovering approximately N942 million allegedly paid out as fraudulent salaries during a payroll verification exercise.

item.title: M-KOPA reaches 10 million customers, six years after its first million
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: The company said on Wednesday that it now onboards about 10,000 customers daily as it scales its lending business beyond its Kenyan roots.

item.title: Fubara Backs Tinubu’s Re-Election, Declares Support For Rivers Rainbow Coalition
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Rivers State governor, Siminalayi Fubara, has formally declared his support for the Rivers Rainbow Coalition ahead of the 2027 general elections, endorsing President Bola Tinubu for a second term and calling on his supporters to back all candidates presented by the political movement. The Rainbow Co

item.title: How To Choose Safer Football Betting Sites: Lessons From Norwegian Comparison Guides
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Every football fan knows the feeling. You have watched the Super Eagles carve open an opponent, you have studied the NPFL form table, and you are ready to place a bet on the weekend’s big match. But then you open a betting site and the odds look suspiciously low, the bonus terms read like a [...] Th

item.title: This is the ideal retirement strategy if you want to keep your healthcare costs down
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Plus, more ways to lower how much you pay out of pocket for medical care in retirement.

item.title: Do I use $300,000 of my $1.2 million retirement savings so my daughter can attend her dream college?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “Our daughter is a highly ranked competitive softball pitcher with a 3.94 GPA.”

item.title: Mamdani U-turn on threat to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The New York City mayor instead called on US authorities to act on International Criminal Court's arrest warrant against the Israeli prime minister.

item.title: Global oil prices hold gains after topping $95 a barrel for the first time in 6 weeks as hopes dim for de-escalation of Iran war
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil prices were holding on to their gains after climbing to a six-week high on Wednesday morning after the U.S. struck Iran for the 11th night in a row and President Donald Trump warned that the U.S. will bomb Iranian bridges and power plants if Tehran shoots at ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Zambia commissions $70 million solar power plant to strengthen energy security
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: Zambia has commissioned its 100-megawatt (MW) Chisamba Phase II Solar Power Plant, projected to increase its electricity generation…

item.title: PenCom DG says proposed pension contribution increase applies only to employers
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Director-General of the National Pension Commission (PenCom), Ms. Omolola Oloworaran, has clarified that the proposed increase in pension contribution rates under the ongoing review of the Pension Reform Act (PRA) 2014 will apply only to employers and not employees. The post PenCom DG says propo

item.title: Japan May Move to Managing Yields, Not Yen, Deutsche Bank Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Japan may have to shift its focus from supporting the yen toward controlling government bond yields if it is to achieve its ambitious economic growth plans, according to Deutsche Bank AG.
